expect: Non-standard model reveals 0 new patterns beyond HMM 3 regimes (quiescent/burst/production)
actual: FALSIFIED — 4 qualitatively new dynamics found that HMM cannot represent
diff: HMM's 3-state Markov assumption creates epistemic lock: misses deterministic structure, intermediate regimes, long-range memory, and volatility clustering
method: RQA + PELT change-point + TDA persistent homology + long-range MI + ARCH volatility

finding_1_deterministic_structure.result: Determinism=0.868 (shuffled baseline=0.764, p<0.0001). Laminarity=0.838 (shuffled=0.640). Max diagonal line=110 sessions.
finding_1_deterministic_structure.new_pattern: YES — session production has significant deterministic structure that stochastic models (including HMM) assume away. The system revisits specific trajectory shapes, not just emission levels. Trapping time=6.77 means the system gets 'stuck' in recurrent states for ~7 sessions — a dynamic invisible to memoryless emission models.
finding_1_deterministic_structure.hmm_blind_spot: HMM models emissions as i.i.d. draws from state-conditional distributions. RQA shows the trajectory SHAPE matters, not just the level.

finding_2_intermediate_regime.result: 19 regimes detected (vs HMM's 3). K-means on segment means: k=5 gives wcv=0.041 vs k=3 wcv=0.160 — 5 clusters fit 4x better. Three segments (S299-S313 mean=3.73, S325-S369 mean=3.21, S487-S508 mean=3.67) fall between production(1.14) and burst(5.30) with distance>1.5 from any HMM center.
finding_2_intermediate_regime.new_pattern: YES — an 'elevated production' regime (mean~3.2-3.7) exists between HMM's production and burst states. This is NOT a finer-grained version of an existing state — it has distinct variance structure (std~2.6-3.5 vs production std=0.3 or burst std=3.1). The system has at least 5 distinguishable operating modes: dormant(0), low-production(~0.2-0.8), standard-production(~1.0), elevated-production(~3.2-3.7), burst(~5.3+).
finding_2_intermediate_regime.hmm_blind_spot: HMM's BIC selected 3 states because Gaussian emissions with fixed variance per state cannot represent the intermediate regime without overfitting. The constraint is the emission model, not the data.

finding_3_long_range_memory.result: MI at lag-55 still 0.110 (35% of lag-1 MI). Power-law decay R²=0.969 vs exponential R²=0.922. Power exponent=-0.20 (very slow decay).
finding_3_long_range_memory.new_pattern: PARTIAL — MI decays slowly, consistent with long-range dependence. HMM (Markov) predicts exponential MI decay; power-law fits slightly better. At lag-55 sessions, the system still retains 35% of its lag-1 predictability. This suggests era-level memory that spans dozens of sessions, beyond what 3-state Markov transitions can capture.
finding_3_long_range_memory.hmm_blind_spot: HMM's Markov property means memory is O(1) — current state screens off all history. The slow MI decay suggests conditioning on multiple past sessions improves prediction beyond what current-state alone provides.

finding_4_volatility_clustering.result: ARCH(1) slope=0.171, p=0.000092. ARCH(5) R²=0.045. Variance of production is autocorrelated — high-variance sessions cluster together.
finding_4_volatility_clustering.new_pattern: YES — volatility itself evolves over time. A burst session makes the NEXT session's variance higher, independent of the mean emission level. This is a second-order dynamic that HMM's constant within-state variance assumption cannot represent.
finding_4_volatility_clustering.hmm_blind_spot: HMM assumes emission variance is fixed per state (e.g., burst always has std=3.1). ARCH shows variance is time-varying even within states — a GARCH-HMM would be needed to capture this.

finding_5_topological_loops.result: 10 persistent H0 clusters (vs HMM's 3 states). 8 persistent H1 loops (max persistence=2.747). H1 features indicate quasi-periodic trajectories in the embedded space.
finding_5_topological_loops.new_pattern: PARTIAL — TDA finds more clusters (10) than HMM states (3), and detects topological loops suggesting quasi-periodic production cycles. The 10 clusters likely reflect distinct trajectory shapes rather than just emission levels. H1 loops suggest the system has cyclic attractor structure (e.g., dormant→ramp→burst→cooldown→dormant).
finding_5_topological_loops.hmm_blind_spot: HMM sees transitions between discrete states but cannot detect the cyclic/periodic structure of those transitions. The loops suggest production follows attracting orbits, not random walks between states.

synthesis.lock_mechanism: HMM's three assumptions (fixed K states, Markov transitions, i.i.d. within-state emissions) each individually block detection of different dynamics: (1) fixed K misses the intermediate regime, (2) Markov misses long-range memory, (3) i.i.d. emissions miss deterministic trajectory structure and volatility clustering.
new_dynamics: Intermediate 'elevated production' regime (mean~3.4, between production and burst)
Long-range memory beyond Markov (MI at lag-55 = 35% of lag-1)

lesson_candidate.summary: Standard stochastic models (Poisson, HMM, USL) create epistemic lock on swarm dynamics — each model's structural assumptions (fixed states, Markov memory, i.i.d. emissions) prevent detection of qualitatively different patterns (deterministic trajectories, intermediate regimes, long-range memory, volatility clustering) that non-standard methods reveal
